1.gl: properly, instrumental music; by implication, a poem set to notes

2.gl: to be high actively, to rise or raise (in various applications, literally or figuratively)

2.gl: probably to brighten up, i.e. (figuratively) be (causatively, make) blithe or gleesome
2.kj: cheer up, be (make) glad, (have, make) joy(-ful), be (make) merry, (cause to, make to) rejoice, [idiom] very.

2.gl: properly, to dangle, i.e. to let down abucket (for drawing out water); figuratively, to deliver
Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
2.kj: Jehovah, the Lord. Compare H3050 (יָהּ), H3069 (יְהֹוִה).

2.dv: or (fully) אוֹיֵב; active participle of H340 (אָיַב);

3.gl: gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a superlative
3.kj: angels, [idiom] exceeding, God (gods) (-dess, -ly), [idiom] (very) great, judges, [idiom] mighty.

3.gl: properly, to mend (by stitching), i.e. (figuratively) to cure
3.kj: cure, (cause to) heal, physician, repair, [idiom] thoroughly, make whole. See H7503 (רָפָה).

3.gl: properly, to be free; but used only causatively and reflexively, to halloo (for help, i.e. freedom from some trouble)
Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
3.kj: Jehovah, the Lord. Compare H3050 (יָהּ), H3069 (יְהֹוִה).

Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
4.kj: Jehovah, the Lord. Compare H3050 (יָהּ), H3069 (יְהֹוִה).

5.gl: physically, to throw (a stone, an arrow) at or away; especially to revere or worship (with extended hands); intensively, to bemoan (by wringing the hands)
5.dv: a primitive root; used only as denominative from H3027 (יָד); literally, to use (i.e. hold out) the hand;

5.kj: give praise, sing forth praises, psalms.

5.gl: a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity

5.gl: a memento, abstractly recollection (rarely if ever); by implication, commemoration
Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
5.kj: Jehovah, the Lord. Compare H3050 (יָהּ), H3069 (יְהֹוִה).
godly (man), good, holy (one), merciful, saint, (un-) godly.
